audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Capernaum house scene, roof opening, aphiemi release, Son of Humanity authority, Levi call, table fellowship, fasting/bridegroom, new cloth/wineskins, Sabbath grainfields, and Son of Humanity as lord of Sabbath.

verses.restored: When he entered Capernaum again after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.
Many gathered, so that there was no longer room, not even at the door; and he was speaking the word to them.
They came bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four.
Not being able to bring him because of the crowd, they removed the roof where he was; after digging through, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man lay.
Seeing their trust, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, Child, your sins/misalignments are released.
Some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts,
Why does this one speak this way? He blasphemes. Who can release sins/misalignments except the Divine alone?
Immediately Jesus, knowing in his spirit that they were reasoning this way within themselves, said to them, Why are you reasoning these things in your hearts?
Which is easier: to say to the paralyzed man, Your sins/misalignments are released, or to say, Rise, take your mat, and walk?
But so that you may know that the Son of Humanity has authority on earth to release sins/misalignments, he said to the paralyzed man,
I say to you: rise, take your mat, and go to your house.
He rose, immediately took the mat, and went out before all, so that all were amazed and glorified the Divine, saying, We never saw anything like this.
He went out again beside the sea; all the crowd came to him, and he taught them.
Passing by, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax office, and said to him, Follow me. He rose and followed him.
As he reclined in his house, many tax collectors and sinners/misaligned ones were reclining with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.
The scribes of the Pharisees, seeing that he ate with sinners/misaligned ones and tax collectors, said to his disciples, Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?
Hearing this, Jesus said to them, The strong do not need a physician, but those having harm. I did not come to call righteous ones, but sinners/misaligned ones.
John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. They came and said to him, Why do John's disciples and the Pharisees' disciples fast, but your disciples do not fast?
Jesus said to them, Can the sons of the bridal chamber fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
Days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.
No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; otherwise the new patch pulls away from the old, and a worse tear happens.
No one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins. But new wine is for fresh wineskins.
It happened that he was passing through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and his disciples began to make their way, plucking heads of grain.
The Pharisees said to him, Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?
He said to them, Have you never read what David did when he had need and was hungry, he and those with him,
how he entered the house of the Divine in the time of Abiathar the high priest and ate the bread of the Presence, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those with him?
He said to them, The Sabbath came to be for the human, not the human for the Sabbath.
So the Son of Humanity is lord even of the Sabbath.
